sourceMovie.title: The Time Machine
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Time Machine for the same reason it broke through in 2002: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Simon Wells keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The science fiction and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Incredible Hulk
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Time Machine clicked for you, The Incredible Hulk (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and adventure, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Time Machine?
faq.answer: Try The Incredible Hulk, Oblivion, Valerian and the City of a Thousand Planets—each shares DNA with The Time Machine while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Time Machine?
faq.answer: The Time Machine (2002) is best described as Science Fiction, Adventure, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Time Machine worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this science fiction lane.
